title: Eliminate DEI in the Military Act
short_title: Eliminate DEI in the Military Act
plain_summary: This bill would stop the military from using government money for diversity, equity, and inclusion programs. It covers all parts of the military, including the Army, Navy, Air Force, and military schools. The ban includes training sessions, educational materials, and jobs focused on diversity topics. If passed, the Department of Defense could no longer spend federal funds on any of these types of activities.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Coast Guard and Maritime Transportation.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Eliminate DEI in the Military Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Prohibition on Use of Funds for Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Activities in the Military
sections.text: The federal government cannot spend any money on DEI activities (diversity, equity, and inclusion activities) in any branch of the Armed Forces, any national service academy, or the Department of Defense. DEI activities include training sessions, programs, educational materials, job positions, and appointments that focus on diversity, equity, and inclusion. National service academies means the United States Military Academy, the United States Naval Academy, the United States Air Force Academy, the United States Coast Guard Academy, and the United States Merchant Marine Academy.
